name: Prefabricated Houses
description:
Prefabricated House — Built in 15 Days, No Foundation Needed

Two different buyers come to this page. A family or individual looking for a faster, simpler alternative to conventional brick construction. Or a site engineer who needs bunkhouses, staff quarters, or site accommodation deployed on a project within days.

Both buyers are in the right place. This page covers both — and routes you to the right product for your specific situation.

Not a Container — What Makes a Prefabricated House Different

A prefabricated house from SAMAN is factory-built and delivered whole to your land — but it is not a shipping container. The structure uses a steel frame with insulated sandwich panels, finished inside and outside to look and feel like a conventional home. If you want the speed and cost savings of factory construction without the corrugated-steel container aesthetic, this is the product.

SAMAN prefabricated house — factory-built steel frame with insulated panel walls, finished to a full residential standard. No container form factor, no corrugated steel — the exterior finish, window placement, and roofline are completely customisable at the design stage.

If you have already decided you want the container form factor — the industrial look, the stacking capability, the ISO fittings — our container houses range covers that product separately.

If you are here because you want a factory-builFor Residential Buyerst home that looks like a home, continue reading.

For Residential Buyers — A Home Built in 15 Days

Conventional brick and mortar construction for a 2BHK home in India takes 8 to 14 months from foundation to handover. You coordinate labourers, manage material deliveries, deal with weather delays, and supervise daily progress — or you pay someone else to do all of that. By the time the house is ready, the original budget has usually grown.

A SAMAN prefabricated house takes 15 working days from order confirmation to delivery at your land. The structure is built at our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility — insulated walls, electrical, windows, doors, and interior lining all fitted at the factory. It arrives complete. You do not manage a construction site.

This model works particularly well for three residential situations: a family building on a plot they already own, a buyer wanting a farmhouse or weekend home on agricultural land outside the city, and a buyer who needs to move into a home quickly without an 18-month construction wait.

Completed SAMAN prefabricated house in a residential garden setting — factory-built, delivered, and placed in 15 working days. Exterior colour, window placement, and door configuration are confirmed at design stage before fabrication begins.

For a home built using the cabin construction method, our portable cabin house subpage covers that configuration in detail.

For Site Buyers — Bunkhouse and Staff Accommodation Deployed in Days

For project managers and site engineers, the prefabricated house is a deployment problem — not a home-buying decision. You need bunkhouses for 10 to 50 workers or staff quarters for a supervisory team. You need them on site within a week. You do not have time for a construction contractor.

A SAMAN prefabricated bunkhouse or staff accommodation unit is fabricated at our factory, transported to your site, and placed within 7 to 21 working days of order confirmation. Each unit comes with electrical provision, insulated walls, windows, and a door. Multiple units can be placed side by side to create an accommodation block for larger teams.

Site buyers do not need to manage foundation work for small configurations — a concrete pad at 4 to 6 support points is all that is required before the unit arrives. We confirm site access requirements before dispatch.

For site accommodation specifically, our bunkhouse cabin subpage covers specifications, layouts, and pricing per unit. For buyers who need a site office in addition to sleeping accommodation, our prefabricated site office covers that configuration.

From Confirmation to Keys — The Delivery Sequence

The 15-day timeline is not an estimate — it is the fabrication and delivery sequence for a standard single or double-unit configuration.

Design confirmation happens in the first two days. You approve the floor plan, window placement, door position, and exterior finish with our team. We share a foundation layout drawing immediately — your concrete pads can be poured while fabrication is underway.

Fabrication at our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility takes the next ten days. Every component — insulated panels, electrical wiring, MCB panel, interior lining, windows, and doors — is fitted at the factory before the unit moves. Nothing is assembled on site.

Quality inspection, loading, and transport take two to three days. For sites within 200 km of either facility, delivery and placement complete on the same day. For more distant sites, transit is factored into the 7 to 21 working day delivery window.

For farmhouse buyers on remote land: before dispatch we confirm road access width and load capacity. Sites with restricted access use a hydraulic placement vehicle instead of a crane. You arrange concrete support pads before we arrive — we manage everything from the factory forward.

What No Foundation Required Actually Means

Conventional brick construction requires a full RCC foundation — continuous concrete footings, plinth beam, and damp-proof course — before a single wall can be built. This foundation work in India typically costs Rs 1.5 to Rs 4 lakh depending on plot size and soil conditions, and takes 3 to 6 weeks before above-ground construction begins.

A SAMAN prefabricated house needs a concrete pad at 4 to 6 support points — one under each corner and at intermediate spans for longer units. For a single 20ft unit, this is 4 poured concrete pads, each approximately 600mm × 600mm × 300mm deep. Total material cost for the pads is typically Rs 8,000 to Rs 20,000 depending on local rates. Total preparation time is 1 to 2 days.

SAMAN prefabricated house installed on a concrete pad foundation on open land. No full RCC plinth required — 4 to 6 concrete support points replace the conventional foundation, saving Rs 1.5 to Rs 4 lakh in civil work before the unit arrives.

For buyers building on agricultural land or remote farmland — where bringing a foundation contractor to site is expensive and logistically difficult — this difference is significant. The structure does not require a civil engineering team before it can be placed. Your land, a flat area, and 4 concrete pads are sufficient.

Prefabricated House vs Brick Construction — The Real Cost

The material and labour cost of brick construction is one number. The total cost is a different number entirely.

A conventional brick 2BHK in India costs Rs 1,500 to Rs 2,500 per sq ft in direct construction materials and labour. On top of that: foundation civil work at Rs 1.5 to Rs 4 lakh, architect and structural engineer fees at Rs 50,000 to Rs 2 lakh, and 8 to 14 months of site supervision — either your own time or a paid supervisor at Rs 15,000 to Rs 30,000 per month. By project end, the real cost of a conventionally built 2BHK is Rs 18 to Rs 32 lakh, not the Rs 15 lakh the initial estimate suggested.

A SAMAN prefabricated house costs Rs 1,200 to Rs 1,800 per sq ft fully fitted — insulated, electrically wired, interior-lined, with windows and doors. For a 600 sq ft two-unit configuration, the total cost is approximately Rs 7.5 to Rs 11 lakh. No foundation civil work beyond the concrete pads. No architect fees for the structure — our design team handles the layout. No 14-month supervision cost. The price you quote is the price you pay.

For site buyers: a 10-unit bunkhouse accommodation block — 600 sq ft of sleeping space — runs Rs 7.5 to Rs 11 lakh total, delivered and placed. No contractor, no foundation team, no site construction management.

Single SAMAN prefabricated unit deployed on open site land — electrical provision, insulated walls, window, and door fitted at the factory before delivery. Multiple units can be placed side by side to create an accommodation block within 7–21 working days.

For buyers evaluating budget options — the quote we provide includes a full breakdown by size, fitout level, and configuration so you can compare before committing.

What SAMAN Delivers — and What You Arrange

Every SAMAN prefabricated house leaves our factory with the following already fitted: 50mm PUF insulated wall and roof panels, internal wall lining, floor, 240V electrical wiring with MCB panel and switchboard, windows with frames, doors with frames, and exterior finish. Nothing is assembled on site — the unit arrives complete.

Completed SAMAN prefabricated house after delivery and placement — exterior panel cladding, white-framed windows, door, and roofing all factory-fitted before dispatch. Utility connections and landscaping are arranged by the buyer after placement.

What you arrange before delivery: the concrete support pads at the marked positions (we provide a foundation layout drawing with your order), crane or hydraulic vehicle access at the site on delivery day (we advise on minimum access road width), and utility connections — electrical connection to your mains and water supply connection to your plumbing — which your local electrician and plumber handle after placement.

For municipal approval: we provide structural drawings, material specifications, and a factory inspection report with every order. These support your building plan application to the local authority. The approval process for a prefabricated house follows the same procedure as conventional construction — same documents, same local body, same timeline.

For site buyers who need to understand deployment responsibilities before committing, our prefab site office subpage covers the site deployment checklist in full.

If You Need Large-Scale Worker Accommodation

Prefabricated houses in this cluster are sized for residential homes and small-scale site accommodation of 1 to 20 units. If your project requires accommodation for 50 to 500 workers — a full labor camp with dormitories, canteen, washrooms, and security facilities — that is a different product at a different scale.

For large-scale worker accommodation, see our labor colony range — factory-built camp infrastructure deployed for EPC, infrastructure, and industrial projects across India.

Frequently Asked Questions

Does a prefabricated house on a farmland plot require the same approvals as a brick house, and does it get listed as a permanent asset?

Yes on both counts. A prefabricated house installed on a concrete foundation on owned land requires a building plan approval from the local municipal authority — the same process as any new residential construction. Once approved and built, it is listed as a permanent structure on property records. For site accommodation on a leased or project site, the structure is classified as a temporary installation and does not require permanent building approval — it is removed at project end.

I need a single-room structure for one or two people on a site — is a prefabricated house the right product or something smaller?

For one to two people requiring a single-room site structure, a prefabricated bunkhouse cabin is the right product — a compact unit with a bed, window, door, and electrical provision. A prefabricated house is a multi-room structure configured for full residential occupancy — bedroom, living area, kitchen, and bathroom. The difference is scale and use: a house for a family or extended stay, a single-room unit for a temporary single-person deployment.

What is the annual maintenance cost of a prefabricated house compared to a brick home?

Annual maintenance cost for a SAMAN prefabricated house is significantly lower than a brick home. Exterior repaint every 6 to 8 years compares to every 3 to 5 years for brick and plaster. Window and door seal inspection every 4 to 5 years is the primary periodic task. For a 600 sq ft home, annual maintenance cost is typically Rs 3,000 to Rs 8,000 — compared to Rs 15,000 to Rs 40,000 for an equivalent brick home including painting cycles. Structural lifespan with standard maintenance is 20 to 25 years minimum.

Can I order 4 bunkhouse units now and add 6 more units to the same site when my project expands?

Yes. SAMAN prefabricated units are manufactured to a consistent specification — a unit added 12 months after the original installation is structurally and dimensionally identical to the first batch. Units are placed side by side on the same concrete pad layout to create an expanded accommodation block. For residential buyers, additional rooms connect to the existing structure at any point without modifying the original unit.

At project end, can the bunkhouse units be recovered and redeployed to my next project site?

Yes. Units installed on concrete pad footings can be crane-lifted, loaded on a flatbed transport, and placed at a new site within 2 to 3 days. The units retain full structural integrity through relocation and can be redeployed 4 to 6 times over their working life. For contractors running multiple concurrent projects, the same units move from site to site rather than being written off at project end.

Tell us your requirement and we will quote within 24 hours.

Share your plot size, number of rooms needed, location, and whether this is residential or site accommodation. Our team responds with a layout recommendation, timeline, and fully itemized quote.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
short_description:
🏡 No container · No brick construction · Factory-built in Bangalore & Greater Noida · Move in or deploy in 15 days

Factory-built prefabricated house for residential home buyers and site accommodation. Steel frame, insulated panels — no foundation required, delivered to your land across Bangalore and Delhi NCR.

Size
Custom · 1RK to 3BHK
Insulation
50mm PUF · All 6 Faces
Delivery
7–21 Working Days
Coverage
Bangalore · Delhi NCR
Brand
SAMAN Portable
Application
Home · Farmhouse · Site Camp

Starting Rs 7.5 lakh · No foundation needed · Structural docs included for approval
